content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662        322-5660           322-5901              322-6441             322-6444

October 25, 1983


Julie Krausse
Joe A. Gonsalves & Son
Park Executive Bldg., Ste. 205
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear Julie:

     This is in reply to your letter dated October 13, 1983,
regarding reporting by lobbyist employers.

     You have asked that I send you a letter detailing what
information needs to be listed on Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist
Employer) under Part III, Section B (Other Payments to Influence
Legislative or Administrative Action), Items 1 and 3.

     Part III, Section B is for reporting payments to influ-
ence, other than payments to a lobbyist and payments which benefit
a reportable person.  Item 1 of Section B is for itemizing such
payments of $25 or more incurred outside the filer's organization,
and Item 2 is for reporting, as a lump sum, such payments of less
than $25.  Generally, the types of payments required to be re-
ported in Items 1 and 2 are payments for goods or services other
than office overhead and normal operating expenses.  Examples of
such payments might include payments to airlines for employee
travel; reimbursements to employees (other than a lobbyist) for
travel expenses; payments to expert witnesses; payments for an
association's annual legislative conference (assuming that no
part of the payment benefited a reportable person); payments in
support of a lobbyist, etc.

     Item 3 of Section B is for reporting costs incurred
within the filer's own organization, such as compensation paid
to employees, and office overhead and operating costs.  Generally,
"overhead" means payments for goods or services which one would
normally think of as necessary to maintain an office, such as
rent, utilities, janitorial services, etc.

     It is impossible to prepare a definitive list of the
types of payments which should be reported under each item of

Julie Krausse
October 25, 1983
Page Two


Section B, but I hope these examples provide you with some
guidance for reporting. If you would like to send me a list
of specific payments, I would be happy to advise you as to
how each payment should be reported.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        Jeanne Pritchard
                                        Political Reform Consultant

Joe A. Gonsalves & Son
PROFESSIONAL LEGISLATIVE REPRESENTATION
PARK EXECUTIVE BLDG • SUITE 205 • 925 L ST • SACRAMENTO, CA 95814-3767 • 916 • 441-0597


October 13, 1983


Jean Pritchard
Fair Political Practice Commission
1100 K. Street
Sacramento, California  95814

Dear Jean:

With regard to our telephone conversation of this
date, I relayed the information you gave me
concerning reporting on the Form 650 to Anthony Gonsalves
and he asked that I write you and request this advice
in writing. (Please understand that their has been
some discrepancy in the advice I have been receiving
from the FPPC from person to person.)

Therefore, please send me a letter detailing what
information needs to be listed on the Form 650, under
Section B (Other Payments to Influence Legislative
or Administrative Action) and 3 (Other Overhead).

Thank you for your patience and assistance.

Sincerely,

Julie Krausse

JULIE KRAUSSE
Executive Assistant

sections.question: You have asked that I send you a letter detailing what information needs to be listed on Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist Employer) under Part III, Section B (Other Payments to Influence Legislative or Administrative Action), Items 1 and 3.
sections.conclusion: Part III, Section B is for reporting payments to influence, other than payments to a lobbyist and payments which benefit a reportable person. Item 1 of Section B is for itemizing such payments of $25 or more incurred outside the filer's organization, and Item 2 is for reporting, as a lump sum, such payments of less than $25. Generally, the types of payments required to be reported in Items 1 and 2 are payments for goods or services other than office overhead and normal operating expenses. Item 3 of Section B is for reporting costs incurred within the filer's own organization, such as compensation paid to employees, and office overhead and operating costs.
sections.question_synthetic: What information must be reported on Form 650 for lobbyist employers under Part III, Section B (Other Payments to Influence Legislative or Administrative Action), specifically for Items 1 and 3?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Form 650 requires reporting of payments to influence legislative or administrative action. Item 1 requires itemizing payments of $25 or more outside the organization (excluding office overhead), Item 2 covers payments under $25 as a lump sum, and Item 3 covers internal organizational costs including employee compensation and office overhead. The FPPC provides examples of reportable payments and notes that definitive lists cannot be provided without specific circumstances.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (October 13, 1983) from Julie Krausse and the FPPC response letter (October 25, 1983) from Jeanne Pritchard. Question and conclusion text extracted from the FPPC response. Signature on response is partially illegible due to handwriting quality.

QUESTION: You have asked that I send you a letter detailing what information needs to be listed on Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist Employer) under Part III, Section B (Other Payments to Influence Legislative or Administrative Action), Items 1 and 3.

CONCLUSION: Part III, Section B is for reporting payments to influence, other than payments to a lobbyist and payments which benefit a reportable person. Item 1 of Section B is for itemizing such payments of $25 or more incurred outside the filer's organization, and Item 2 is for reporting, as a lump sum, such payments of less than $25. Generally, the types of payments required to be reported in Items 1 and 2 are payments for goods or services other than office overhead and normal operating expenses. Item 3 of Section B is for reporting costs incurred within the filer's own organization, such as compensation paid to employees, and office overhead and operating costs.

embedding.summary: FPPC Political Reform Consultant Jeanne Pritchard advises a lobbyist employer on how to complete Form 650 (Report of Lobbyist Employer), specifically clarifying which payments to report under Part III, Section B for other payments to influence legislative or administrative action.
